creator: beno0028@umn.edu
text:
I apologize for the rookie mistake with linebreaks on the web form.  The
"Description" and "Fix" fields, with the correct links to the referenced
commits, are appended below.

With thanks,
Scott Benolkin


Description:

On 29 August 2005 the port shells/zsh-devel was removed
(http://www.freebsd.org/cgi/getmsg.cgi?fetch=1078555+0+/usr/local/www/db/text/2005/cvs-all/20050904.cvs-all).

Perhaps as a consequence, on 30 April 2006 the port shells/zsh was
changed from version 4.2.6 of the stable branch to version 4.3.2 of the
zsh development branch
(http://www.freebsd.org/cgi/getmsg.cgi?fetch=551139+0+/usr/local/www/db/text/2006/cvs-all/20060507.cvs-all).

This new version, as any developmental software may, seems in practice
to have more bugs and regressions compared to the previous (stable)
branch.  More importantly, the potential exists for more bugs and
regressions in the future.

Fix:

The solution, in my opinion, is to reinstate the shells/zsh-devel port
for the development branch and have the stable branch on shells/zsh.
This would likely be better for users, whose subsequent experiences
would be more appropriate to their choices, and the maintainer, for whom
ad hoc patches would be less necessary, perhaps unnecessary, for
continued maintenance of each port.
